stitch_shortlist.author: lucy_seitz
stitch_shortlist.claim: Heat damage forced me to cut my hair

rubric.miss: 4.5d old — just past the <4d rising window; still pacing 324K/day

pitch.the_claim: Heat styling wrecked my hair so badly I had to chop it off.
pitch.why_it_stitches: 1.46M views in her under-used HAIR lane; a half-right claim - heat damage is real, but 'you must cut it' skips prevention and bond repair.
pitch.your_fit: She's fellowship-trained in lasers and posts hair-health (oily-hair mistakes 1.7M; erythema ab igne heat-damage rash 225K). Split ends can't be glued back, but the cuticle can be protected - no prior heat-styling video, so this is fresh territory for her.
angles: empathy-first: the cut wasn't your fault - here's what 450F does to the cuticle
switch-don't-quit: heat protectant + lower temp beats the chop
what heat damage actually is (and the one repair that works)

rubric.miss: exact tip not in caption - watch in-app before drafting to confirm DIY-sunscreen vs a zinc mask

pitch.the_claim: A zinc-oxide 'mask' tip (caption only credits '@Mariana for this tip' - WATCH FIRST to confirm the exact hack).
pitch.why_it_stitches: 1.12M in 2.7d and climbing; zinc oxide is a mineral-sunscreen active - if it's a DIY-sunscreen or DIY-mask hack, that's a clean safety teachable (untested SPF, uneven coverage).
pitch.your_fit: Deep sunscreen corpus (rosacea-safe SPF picks; application myth-busting). Her stance: use a tested, regulated sunscreen - a DIY zinc mix can't be trusted for SPF. Confirm the tip, then draft.
angles: 'you want me to WHAT?': why a DIY zinc mask is not sunscreen
empathy-first: the instinct is good, the execution is risky
what zinc oxide genuinely does for skin

rubric.miss: 4.7d old - past the <4d window; a 'yes-and' amplify, not a correction

pitch.the_claim: 'SPF stan til I die' - a civilian loudly defending chemical SPF 50.
pitch.why_it_stitches: 1.02M views and a RARE pro-sunscreen civilian voice worth amplifying against the 'chemical sunscreen is toxic' wave - a 'yes, and' with the science.
pitch.your_fit: She's pro-sunscreen and debunked 'sunscreen causes cancer' (posted 4d ago). Position: chemical filters are safe, well-studied, and the best sunscreen is the one you'll actually reapply.
angles: 'yes, and': the science on why chemical SPF is safe
the best sunscreen is the one you'll reapply
mineral vs chemical - both work, pick for your skin

stitch_shortlist.author: thebodyytemple
stitch_shortlist.claim: Don't fear 'heavy' sunscreen if you skip moisturizer

pitch.the_claim: You can't worry about 'heavy' sunscreen while you skip moisturizer entirely.
pitch.why_it_stitches: 612K in 2.1d and rising; half-right - sunscreen isn't 'heavy/bad,' and moisturizer + SPF aren't an either/or.
pitch.your_fit: She teaches the AM routine as moisturizer + sunscreen (or a moisturizing SPF for dry skin). Reframe 'heavy' as 'wrong texture for your skin type.'
angles: empathy-first: 'heavy' just means the wrong formula for you
moisturizer AND SPF - the 2-step AM order
dry skin? a moisturizing sunscreen does both jobs

stitch_shortlist.author: drjessicaburgy
stitch_shortlist.claim: Nail melanoma looks like a dark band, not a blotch

rubric.miss: 353K is modest, but pacing 208K/day and it's her exact specialty

pitch.the_claim: Nail melanoma more often shows as a dark vertical band than a blotch - and she's right.
pitch.why_it_stitches: 353K in <2d in her EXACT specialty; a fellow derm being correct -> 'yes, and' expansion, never a takedown.
pitch.your_fit: Verbatim corpus: she 'really cares if you have a new brown spot on your nail that has been there more than 6 weeks and is not growing out.' As a Mohs surgeon she can add Hutchinson's sign, the 6-week rule, and when to biopsy.
angles: 'yes, and': add the 6-week rule + Hutchinson's sign
as a Mohs surgeon, here's the nail streak I biopsy
one dark nail band that's benign vs one that's urgent
